name: Ryne Schroder
headline: Engineering leader with experience spanning a $200M construction program at ESCO, product and platform ownership at Vendrix, and later data, operations, and AI systems on GCP.
availability: Open to senior/staff platform engineering, data architecture, and cloud plus AI systems roles.
summary: At ESCO, I managed a $200M project, directly managed 8 engineers, and coordinated delivery with the vice president of ESCO and client representatives.

At Vendrix, I managed one engineer while leading frontend and backend delivery for the v2 release, including ERP integrations and a transaction-tagging model that let customers link spend to budgets, projects, resources, and equipment. Foundation Software later acquired Vendrix.
After Vendrix, I applied the same operating discipline to EdgeWatch-style field systems, governed data platforms, and grounded AI knowledge systems for consulting clients.
focusAreas: Engineering management, delivery ownership, and stakeholder coordination
Vendrix platform and product work: v2 release leadership, ERP integrations, tagging schemas, and GCP operations
Governed data platforms, BigQuery, replay/backfills, and analytics
Grounded AI knowledge systems with citations and evaluation
signalsOfFit: ESCO: managed a $200M project, 8 direct reports, and executive/client coordination
Vendrix: managed 1 engineer while leading v2 frontend and backend delivery, ERP integrations, and transaction-tagging design
Vendrix: GCP platform ownership, App Engine to Cloud Run migration, CI/CD, IAM, KMS, logging, and monitoring
EdgeWatch: offline-first PWA workflows, Raspberry Pi telemetry, alert routing, and operator visibility
Data platforms and AI: event-driven ingestion, BigQuery, schema drift handling, replay/backfills, retrieval with citations, and evaluation

about.title: Civil engineering, agriculture, and software delivery all shaped the way I lead and build.
intro: I came into software through civil engineering and real-world operations, so I tend to think first about constraints, failure modes, coordination, and what happens when a system has to work outside a perfect demo.

That showed up early at ESCO, where I managed a $200M project, directly managed 8 engineers, and worked with the vice president of ESCO and client representatives to keep delivery, reporting, and risk aligned.

It carried forward at Vendrix, where I managed one engineer while owning the v2 frontend and backend release, ERP integrations, transaction-tagging design, and the GCP platform work behind a construction finance product that Foundation Software later acquired.
personal: My background in agriculture and civil engineering gave me a lasting respect for systems that have to survive real operating conditions, not just good intentions.
That is still how I approach software engineering: build for operators, analysts, stakeholders, and customers who need something dependable, understandable, and useful when the stakes are real.
qualifications: Engineering management with direct reports, executive coordination, client communication, and ownership of large delivery scopes.
Hands-on software engineering across frontend, backend, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, IAM, observability, migrations, and operational hardening.
Product and platform leadership spanning ERP integrations, event-driven systems, schema design, release management, and secure cloud delivery on GCP.
Data and AI architecture covering BigQuery platforms, schema-drift handling, replay and backfill workflows, forecasting, and grounded retrieval systems with citations and evaluation.
currentFocus: Most recently, as an independent consultant, I have been building software systems for real estate, law, and agriculture clients: event-driven data platforms, forecasting systems, grounded AI knowledge tools, and offline-first operational software with edge telemetry.

Those engagements have kept me current in modern software engineering across TypeScript, Python, GCP, Terraform, Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, BigQuery, monitoring, and security controls while staying close to business operations.
about.closing: I am at my best in roles that need both leadership and execution: someone who can manage scope and stakeholders, design the architecture, and still do the implementation work required to ship.
